channel.title: Yugabyte, Inc. — recent CVEs
channel.description: Most recent CVEs assigned by Yugabyte, Inc. (yugabyte).

item.title: CVE-2026-1966 · 2.4 LOW — YugabyteDB Anywhere Exposes LDAP Credentials in Cleartext in Web UI
item.description: YugabyteDB Anywhere displays LDAP bind passwords configured via gflags in cleartext within the web UI. An authenticated user with access to the configuration view could obtain LDAP credentials, potentially enabling unauthorized access to external dir…

item.title: CVE-2025-8866 · 5.1 MEDIUM
item.description: YugabyteDB Anywhere web server does not properly enforce authentication for the /metamaster/universe API endpoint. An unauthenticated attacker could exploit this flaw to obtain server networking configuration details, including private and public IP…

item.title: CVE-2025-8865 · 4.1 MEDIUM
item.description: The YugabyteDB tablet server contains a flaw in its YCQL query handling that can trigger a null pointer dereference when processing certain malformed inputs. An authenticated attacker could exploit this issue to crash the YCQL tablet server, resultin…

item.title: CVE-2025-8864 · 6.8 MEDIUM
item.description: Shared Access Signature token is not masked in the backup configuration response and is also exposed in the yb_backup logs

item.title: CVE-2025-8863 · 7 HIGH 🌩️
item.description: YugabyteDB diagnostic information was transmitted over HTTP, which could expose sensitive data during transmission

item.title: CVE-2025-8862 · 7 HIGH 🌩️
item.description: YugabyteDB has been collecting diagnostics information from YugabyteDB servers, which may include sensitive gflag configurations. To mitigate this, we recommend upgrading the database to a version where this information is properly redacted.

item.title: CVE-2024-11193 · 6.5 MEDIUM
item.description: An information disclosure vulnerability exists in Yugabyte Anywhere, where the LDAP bind password is logged in plaintext within application logs. This flaw results in the unintentional exposure of sensitive information in Yugabyte Anywhere logs, pote…

item.title: CVE-2024-11165 · 5.7 MEDIUM
item.description: An information disclosure vulnerability exists in the backup configuration process where the SAS token is not masked in the configuration response. This oversight results in sensitive information leakage within the yb_backup log files, exposing the S…

item.title: CVE-2024-6908 · 6 MEDIUM — Admin Can Escalate Privileges to SuperAdmin Using Manual PUT Request
item.description: Improper privilege management in Yugabyte Platform allows authenticated admin users to escalate privileges to SuperAdmin via a crafted PUT HTTP request, potentially leading to unauthorized access to sensitive system functions and data.

item.title: CVE-2024-6895 · 6.1 MEDIUM — Insecure Account Profile Management
item.description: Insufficient authentication in user account management in Yugabyte Platform allows local network attackers with a compromised user session to change critical security information without re-authentication. An attacker with user session and access to…

item.title: CVE-2024-0006 · 5.4 MEDIUM — DB User Password Leak in Application Log
item.description: Information exposure in the logging system in Yugabyte Platform allows local attackers with access to application logs to obtain database user credentials in log files, potentially leading to unauthorized database access.

item.title: CVE-2023-6002 · 6.5 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Log Injection
item.description: YugabyteDB is vulnerable to cross site scripting (XSS) via log injection. Writing invalidated user input to log files can allow an unprivileged attacker to forge log entries or inject malicious content into the logs.

item.title: CVE-2023-6001 · 5.3 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Prometheus Metrics Accessible Pre-Authentication
item.description: Prometheus metrics are available without authentication. These expose detailed and sensitive information about the YugabyteDB Anywhere environment.

item.title: CVE-2023-4640 · 6.5 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Set Logging Level Without Authentication
item.description: The controller responsible for setting the logging level does not include any authorization checks to ensure the user is authenticated. This can be seen by noting that it extends Controller rather than AuthenticatedController and includes no further…

item.title: CVE-2023-0575 · 7.2 HIGH — Remote Code Execution
item.description: External Control of Critical State Data, Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection') vulnerability in YugaByte, Inc. Yugabyte DB on Windows, Linux, MacOS, iOS (DevopsBase.Java:execCommand, TableManager.Java:runCommand modules) allows AP…

item.title: CVE-2023-0745 · 6.7 MEDIUM — Arbitrary File Write in High Availability Backup Upload
item.description: The High Availability functionality of Yugabyte Anywhere can be abused to write arbitrary files through the backup upload endpoint by using path traversal characters. This vulnerability is associated with program files PlatformReplicationManager.Java…

item.title: CVE-2023-0574 · 6.8 MEDIUM — Server-Side Request Forgery
item.description: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF), Improperly Controlled Modification of Dynamically-Determined Object Attributes, Improper Restriction of Excessive Authentication Attempts vulnerability in YugaByte, Inc. Yugabyte Managed allows Accessing Functional…

item.title: CVE-2022-37397 · 8.3 HIGH — The software is vulnerable when using LDAP-based authentication in YCQL with Microsoft’s Active Directory
item.description: An issue was discovered in the YugabyteDB 2.6.1 when using LDAP-based authentication in YCQL with Microsoft’s Active Directory. When anonymous or unauthenticated LDAP binding is enabled, it allows bypass of authentication with an empty password.
